meta.title: Lev MVP Build Report
meta.description: A launch-candidate build report for Leviathan: daemon, tmux, graph, FlowMind, proofs, exec, and MVP readiness.

props.title: Leviathan is in launch-candidate territory.
props.subtitle: The runtime proof spine is green, tmux is real, FlowMind is carrying the control plane, and the MVP is down to final hardening gates.
props.meta: North Star: One runtime. Every agent. Every surface.

props.content: This is no longer a pile of parts. Over the last two weeks, Lev crossed the line from architecture thesis into a working agent runtime: events can be inspected and projected, exec produces certifying receipts, tmux sessions are controlled as first-class workers, Pentagon gates are green for the proof spine, and Now/AgentPing surfaces are carrying real operator state. The launch posture is simple: proof-spine ready, final gates left.

props.label: Launch Pulse
props.title: The build is hot. The proof is real.

props.label: Default daemon Run Fabric gate green
Observed green package tests in proof state
AgentFS receipts indexed in live audit
Executive State
props.title: 98 percent energy, proof-gated release discipline
props.subtitle: The MVP story is strong because the runtime is now proving itself through the same rails developers will use.

props.title: Proof spine is green
props.content: runtime-lifecycle-exec-proof-spine reports s5_default_daemon_run_fabric_green. S4 real exec is certified by rcpt-8a4f95daa123b2a2 / exec 313c4d38b2e2; default and named Pentagon gates pass with diagnostics empty.

props.title: Manual events mode is closed
props.content: lev events tail, lev triggers project --dry-run, and lev triggers dispatch --exec-dry-run prove the manual inspection rail. Daemon mode is now a successor, not a blind leap.

props.title: Final launch gates are known
props.content: The remaining launch gap is not conceptual: finish security P0 hardening, unify event provenance policy, automate the daemon loop, and validate the remaining MVP-critical tasks.

props.label: Proof Spine
props.title: The runtime now has a certifying backbone

rows.meaning: Provider-backed managed tmux execution completed with receipt and trace proof.
Provider discovery and Poly evidence now satisfy the named gate.
The default daemon-oriented proof path is green enough to begin automation wiring.
The core proof task is structurally ready and receipt-linked.

rows.evidence: workstream, lifecycle, FlowMind, orchestration, harness, event-dispatch, graph, poly, exec, testing, daemon-pentagon
rows.meaning: The runtime stack has broad package-level regression coverage in the current proof state.

props.label: Events Daemon Plan
props.title: Manual first was the right call
Current event truth
props.content: .lev/events.jsonl is a project-local projection used by daemon and exec paths. Other logs exist today: ~/.local/share/lev/events.jsonl, ~/.local/share/lev/daemon/state.jsonl, and .lev/agentfs/exec/events.jsonl. event-dispatch reads a JSONL source; it does not own ingestion.

props.title: Daemon target
props.content: For the MVP hot path, keep one ordered project event stream for replay simplicity, then treat specialized logs as evidence/projection stores. The daemon should automate the manual rail: observe, project, dispatch through SDK/exec, and emit one linked receipt.

props.label: Exec Testing
props.title: How the exec portion gets proven

rows.proof: Trigger registration, projection, and dispatch behavior at core/event-dispatch.
.lev/agentfs/exec/events.jsonl records exec evidence and failure paths.
3 matched flows produce dry-run exec receipts with daemon=false and event_bus_started=false.

rows.test: lev exec against existing PM task DAG
rows.proof: rcpt-8a4f95daa123b2a2 certifies provider-backed managed tmux execution.
Default daemon Run Fabric gate passes diagnostics=[].

props.content:
PATH=$PWD/node_modules/.bin:$PATH lev events tail --source .lev/pm/tasks/runtime-lifecycle-exec-proof-spine-v1/fixtures/events/manual-projection-event.jsonl --limit 1 --json
PATH=$PWD/node_modules/.bin:$PATH lev triggers project .lev/pm/tasks/runtime-lifecycle-exec-proof-spine-v1/fixtures/events/manual-projection-event.jsonl --dry-run --json
PATH=$PWD/node_modules/.bin:$PATH lev triggers dispatch .lev/pm/tasks/runtime-lifecycle-exec-proof-spine-v1/fixtures/events/manual-projection-event.jsonl --exec-dry-run --json
PATH=$PWD/node_modules/.bin:$PATH lev pentagon gate --project . --json

props.label: MVP / Roadmap / Vision
props.title: The thesis is still the same. The proof is finally catching up.

props.content:
**North Star:** One runtime. Every agent. Every surface.

**Vision:** FlowMind is the substrate, Poly is the protocol/runtime boundary, AgentPing is the human-loop surface, and AgentGuard/policy closes the authority loop.

**MVP reality:** Manual event projection, real exec, tmux worker control, receipt/trace lookup, and Pentagon promotion are now credible. The release gate is operational hardening, not product imagination.
Agents should not be trapped in chat windows. Lev turns intent into governed graph patches, executable FlowMind programs, provider-backed runs, and receipt-backed evidence across every surface.

flowchart LR
  A[Human intent] --> B[Stream of consciousness router]
  B --> C[Graph patch]
  C --> D[FlowMind gates]
  D --> E[Poly / SDK dispatch]
  E --> F[lev exec]
  F --> G[tmux / provider workers]
  G --> H[Receipts and traces]
  H --> I[Pentagon promotion]
  I --> J[AgentPing surfaces]

props.label: Final Gates
props.title: What has to close before public launch

rows.why: OSS launch cannot ship ambient authority or unsafe execution paths.
rows.closure: mvp-security-p0-burndown-v1 validates with receipts and current audit/gate output.

rows.why: Automation should only wrap the proven manual rail.
rows.closure: daemon receipt links event_id, flow_id, exec_id, receipt_id, trace_id; replay produces identical projections.

rows.why: MVP should keep one hot project event stream for ordering while specialized logs remain evidence stores.
rows.closure: Proven writer matrix and no split-brain dispatch source.

rows.why: The first install/demo needs a single obvious FlowMind path.
rows.closure: context intake -> proposal/design/spec checks -> gates -> receipts, no renderer-local execution.

rows.why: Product surfaces are real; visual QA needs to be mandatory, not heroic.
rows.closure: browser screenshot checks and review readiness receipts on every dashboard/report.

rows.why: The outside world needs install, doctor, first run, and proof in one path.
rows.closure: Fresh clone to first receipt succeeds without private context.

props.label: Bottom Line
props.title: The MVP is not waiting on a miracle anymore.
Launch posture
props.content: Leviathan is proof-spine ready and product-surface credible. The next work is launch discipline: finish the P0 security gate, automate events without hiding manual truth, close the starter SDLC flow, and ship a public quickstart that produces a real receipt.
